title: surfacea bracteata
intro: Surfacea bracteata, also known as the yellow indigo or yellow jessamine, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its woody vines and clusters of small, yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Surfacea bracteata include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Surfacea bracteata can be prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine as a treatment for various ailments, including fever and skin conditions.
